faqSubtitle: Here's what people ask us most about PNG to PDF conversion.
faq1Question: Is this PNG to PDF converter really free?
faq1Answer: Yes, completely free. No hidden costs, no watermarks on your PDF, and no account required. Just upload your images and download your PDF.
faq2Question: What image formats can I convert to PDF?
faq2Answer: We support PNG, JPG (JPEG), and WEBP. If you have images in other formats like BMP or TIFF, you'll need to convert them first — though PNG and JPG cover the vast majority of use cases.
faq3Question: Will my images lose quality when converted to PDF?
faq3Answer: No. We preserve the original quality of every image. There's no re-compression or quality loss during the PDF creation process. Your images will look exactly the same inside the PDF as they do as standalone files.
faq4Question: Can I control the page size and orientation?
faq4Answer: Absolutely. Before converting, you can choose between A4, Letter, and Legal page sizes, or use the 'Fit to Image' option that sizes each page to match the image dimensions exactly. You can also set the orientation to portrait, landscape, or let the tool decide automatically.
faq5Question: How many images can I combine into one PDF?
faq5Answer: You can combine up to 50 images in a single conversion. Each image becomes one page in the PDF, in whatever order you arrange them. If you need to merge more than 50 images, you can convert in batches and then merge the resulting PDFs using our Merge PDF tool.
seoTitle: Why Convert PNG Images to PDF?
seoPara1: PDF is the universal format for sharing documents. It looks the same on every device, can't be accidentally edited, and is accepted everywhere — from email attachments to professional submissions. When you have a collection of PNG or JPG images that need to travel together, converting them to a single PDF is almost always the right move. It keeps everything organized, reduces the number of files you need to manage, and makes sharing a one-click action.
seoPara2: Our converter is built for people who care about the end result. We preserve the full resolution of your images and let you control exactly how they're laid out in the document. Whether you want each image to fill an A4 page, fit a Letter-size layout, or simply match its natural dimensions, the choice is yours. And because the whole process runs in your browser, there's nothing to download and no subscription to sign up for.
seoSubtitle: Common Ways People Use This Tool
seoPara3: Students scan handwritten notes and combine them into a single PDF for submission. Photographers compile proofs for client review. Designers package mockups into a clean presentation deck. Small business owners assemble receipts and invoices into a single PDF for accounting. Real estate agents combine property photos into a shareable document. Whatever your use case, the process is the same: upload, arrange, convert, done.
